sections.paragraph:
προσπορεύομαι dep. (Aristot., Polyb.; SIG 344, 112; PEleph 18, 5 [223/222 b.c.]; PMagd 27, 6; PAmh 33, 17; UPZ 79, 3 [159 b.c.] προσπορεύεταί μοι; LXX) to come up to, approach τινί Mk 10:35.—JLee, Equivocal and Stereotyped Renderings in the LXX: RB 87, ’80, 104–17. DELG s.v. πόρος. M-M.

dep. dep. = deponent

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PEleph PEleph = Elephantine-Papyri—List 4

PMagd PMagd = Papyrus de Magdôla—List 4

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

RB RB = Revue Biblique—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 884
